preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiced palato-alveolar fricative is a form of assibilation and palatalization, where a rhotic sound is strengthened and shifted toward the palate.

examples.note: In many highland dialects, the rhotic (especially the trill, but also the tap) is realized as a voiced fricative sounding like 'zh'.
Historical palatalized rhotic assibilated into a voiced retroflex/palatal sibilant (spelled <rz>).

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
